annotations.label: Arithmetic function

anchor.snippet: is generally any function whose domain is the set of positive integers

annotations.note: `ArithmeticFunction R` is a function `ℕ → R` with `f 0 = 0`, generalizing the codomain beyond ℂ.

annotations.label: Divisor function example

anchor.snippet: An example of an arithmetic function is the divisor function

annotations.note: `ArithmeticFunction.sigma k` is the divisor (power-sum) function σ_k.

annotations.label: Completely additive function

anchor.snippet: completely additive if

annotations.note: Mathlib has no `IsAdditive`/`IsCompletelyAdditive` predicate for arithmetic functions.

annotations.label: Completely multiplicative function

anchor.snippet: completely multiplicative if

annotations.note: There is no named predicate; completely multiplicative functions are represented as monoid-with-zero homs `ℕ →*₀ R` (e.g. in EulerProduct lemmas).

annotations.label: Coprime integers

anchor.snippet: Two whole numbers m and n are called coprime

annotations.note: `Nat.Coprime m n` is defined as `gcd m n = 1`.

annotations.label: Additive function

anchor.snippet: additive if a ( mn ) = a ( m ) + a ( n ) for all coprime natural numbers

annotations.note: No `IsAdditive` predicate on `ArithmeticFunction` exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Multiplicative function

anchor.snippet: multiplicative if a (1) = 1 and a ( mn ) = a ( m ) a ( n ) for all coprime natural numbers

annotations.note: `IsMultiplicative f` is `f 1 = 1 ∧ ∀ {m n}, m.Coprime n → f (m*n) = f m * f n`.

annotations.label: Divisor sum σk(n)

anchor.section: σ k ( n ), τ ( n ), d ( n ) – divisor sums

annotations.note: `sigma 1 n = ∑ d ∈ divisors n, d`, the case k=1 of `ArithmeticFunction.sigma`.

anchor.section: σ k ( n ), τ ( n ), d ( n ) – divisor sums
anchor.snippet: is therefore the number of (positive) divisors of n

annotations.note: `sigma 0 n = #n.divisors`, the number-of-divisors function (k=0 of σ).

annotations.label: Dirichlet characters completely multiplicative

anchor.snippet: All Dirichlet characters χ ( n ) are completely multiplicative.

annotations.note: `DirichletCharacter R n := MulChar (ZMod n) R`, and `MulChar.map_mul` gives `χ(ab)=χ(a)χ(b)` for all arguments.

anchor.snippet: The principal character (mod n ) is denoted by

annotations.note: The principal/trivial character is the multiplicative identity `1 : DirichletCharacter R n`, but there is no dedicated `principalCharacter` definition.

annotations.label: Legendre symbol

anchor.snippet: is the Legendre symbol , defined for all integers a and all odd primes

annotations.note: `legendreSym p a` is the Legendre symbol for prime `p`.

anchors.snippet: is the Legendre symbol , defined for all integers a and all odd primes

anchors.value: {\displaystyle \left({\frac {a}{p}}\right)={\begin{cases}\;\;\,0&{\text{if }}a\equiv 0{\pmod {p}},\\+1&{\text{if }}a\not \equiv 0{\pmod {p}}{\text{ and for some integer }}x,\;a\equiv x^{2}{\pmod {p}}\\-1&{\text{if there is no such }}x.\end{cases}}}

annotations.label: ω(n) is additive

anchor.snippet: defined above as the number of distinct primes dividing n , is additive

annotations.note: `cardDistinctFactors_mul : m.Coprime n → ω (m*n) = ω m + ω n`.

annotations.label: Ω(n) is completely additive

anchor.snippet: defined above as the number of prime factors of n counted with multiplicities, is completely additive

annotations.note: `cardFactors_mul : m≠0 → n≠0 → Ω (m*n) = Ω m + Ω n` (no coprimality needed).

annotations.label: νp(n) is completely additive

anchor.section: ν p ( n ) – p -adic valuation of an integer n
anchor.snippet: defined above as the exponent of the largest power of p dividing n , is completely additive

annotations.note: `padicValNat.mul : a≠0 → b≠0 → padicValNat p (a*b) = padicValNat p a + padicValNat p b`.
